source.title: Structural Damage - Crane Other Lifting - Ram Roller Winch Mooring Line

actions_taken: Evacuated top of SW hull column
Assessed mooring line status via CCTV and marine system data
Decided not to muster or shut in facility based on damage assessment
Implemented temporary securement of inboard chain
Repositioned guide roller assembly back into housing
Achieved storm safe condition by 23 May 2020
Suspended all mooring system maintenance pending causal investigation results
event.summary: On 29 January 2020 at 1400 hours, a Roller Ram Winch (RRW) mooring chain incident occurred at Shell Offshore Inc's Appomattox platform in Mississippi Canyon Block 437. During maintenance activities on mooring line L-11, the chain links suddenly began paying out from the chain locker. The guide roller broke free from its bearing cradle, and 22 chain links paid out outboard. The chain stopper cylinders, angle transmitter, and Slack Chain Hang-off Unit (SCHU) stopper were forced out of frame. The root cause was identified as human performance error: the maintenance crew assumed the mooring chain was in lock position without visually confirming it. Contributing factors included flawed equipment design lacking proximity switches and programmed interlocks to confirm SCHU stopper extension. Total repair and recovery costs were $5,750,000.
recommendations: BSEE to provide training on mooring for large platforms
Update operating manual to identify how and when load is transferred to SCHU
Strengthen and clarify roles and responsibilities in maintenance procedures
Install additional proximity switches and programmed interlocks to confirm SCHU stoppers are fully extended
Permanently reinstate gripper used during installation/commissioning as secondary barrier to prevent unplanned chain payout

threats.name: Inadequate verification of chain lock position
Design gap and imbalance in guide roller stoppers
Absence of proximity switches and interlocks
Lack of secondary barrier to prevent chain payout

consequences.name: Structural damage to Roller Ram Winch assembly
Potential hull damage from loose chain in rough seas

controls.name: Visual verification of chain lock position
controls.barrier_role: Procedural check to confirm mooring chain is secured
controls.lod_basis: Primary human-based control to prevent unplanned payout

supporting_text: The MGP maintenance crew assumed the mooring chain was in the lock position when work was completed. The MGP maintenance crew did not visually observe the chain in a lock position.
the MGP's maintenance crew failed to check if the RRW roller assembly upper chain stopper pawls were engaged

controls.name: Proximity switches and programmed interlocks for SCHU stopper confirmation
controls.barrier_role: Automated detection and prevention of chain payout when stoppers not fully extended
controls.lod_basis: Automated engineering control to prevent unplanned payout

supporting_text: The mooring line's design did not have proximity switches and programmed interlocks to confirm SCHU stoppers are fully extended.
Shell identified the need for updating the design modifications to install additional proximity switches and programmed interlocks to confirm SCHU stoppers are fully extended.

controls.name: Gripper secondary barrier for chain payout prevention
controls.barrier_role: Secondary mechanical barrier to prevent unplanned chain payout
controls.lod_basis: Secondary engineering control to prevent chain payout

supporting_text: Shell permanently reinstated a gripper used during installation/ commissioning as a secondary barrier to prevent unplanned payout of chain.

controls.name: Maintenance procedure with clear roles and responsibilities
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure proper execution of mooring system maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure maintenance crew competency and coordination

supporting_text: Shell identified the need for updating the maintenance procedures to strengthen/clarify roles and responsibilities for each member of the maintenance team.

controls.name: Operating manual documentation of load transfer to SCHU
controls.barrier_role: Procedural documentation to ensure maintenance crew understands load transfer mechanism
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure crew knowledge of system operation

supporting_text: updating the operating manual to identify how and when load is transferred to SCHU

controls.name: Post-incident evacuation and area isolation
controls.barrier_role: Immediate response to isolate personnel from hazard area
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation control to prevent personnel injury from ongoing chain payout

supporting_text: Following the incident, Shell's personnel on the deck called the control room to confirm all involved workers on the top of hull were accounted for. The top of the SW hull column was evacuated and the Offshore Installation Manager (OIM) was notified.
red barricade tape restricted access to the SW Hull column only to personnel performing the maintenance activities on the mooring system

controls.name: CCTV inspection and marine system data analysis for damage assessment
controls.barrier_role: Detection and assessment of damage to inform operational decisions
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation control to assess extent of damage and remaining mooring line integrity

supporting_text: data analysis from the marine system confirming the satisfactory status of the remaining mooring lines, the underwater chain stoppers, and the tension and departure angle of the mooring lines
reviewing the damage on the top of the SW hull with closed circuit tv cameras

controls.name: Temporary securement of inboard chain to prevent further payout
controls.barrier_role: Temporary mechanical control to stabilize chain and prevent additional payout
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation control to stabilize system pending permanent repairs

supporting_text: Initial temporary securement was planned to anchor the inboard chain to prevent possible pay-out outboard from the chain locker.

controls.name: Storm safe condition achievement with guide roller repositioning and chain stopper engagement
controls.barrier_role: Restoration of mooring system to safe operational state
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation control to restore system functionality and prevent storm-related damage

supporting_text: As of 23 May 2020, Shell's Offshore Execution Team completed the work to make the RRW storm safe. The guide roller assembly had been repositioned into its housing, the RRW had been tested to confirm operability, and the winch was utilized to pay out twenty links of belly chain into the locker with the upper chain stopper pawls engaged.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
